one_line_ask: License one large Adobe Stock architecture photograph, brand-grade it to the Harbor Crest palette, then crop/reframe that single graded master into a desktop hero, tablet hero, mobile hero, two section-header strips, and two promo banner sizes, vectorize the client logo, and package the cohesive web-ready set with a font recommendation and a review board.
brief: Harbor Crest Realty is a boutique waterfront-and-city brokerage whose website structure and copy already work, but the imagery feels dated and generic and no longer signals the premium, trustworthy feel we want. Refresh the visual layer into ONE cohesive, web-ready graphics family, all cut from a single hero look so the site feels intentional. (1) HERO SOURCE: source a high-quality, aspirational real-estate architecture photograph from Adobe Stock — a modern light-filled open living space or a clean contemporary home exterior at golden hour, no recognizable faces, no readable signage or brand marks — and license it at LARGE resolution (we need it big enough to cut multiple crops from, roughly 5000px on the long edge; do NOT stretch or AI-expand a small file). (2) BRAND GRADE: color-grade the licensed photo to our palette — primary deep harbor navy #17324B, warm sand accent #C9A66B, off-white #F5F1E8: auto-tone for a clean baseline, warm the white balance slightly toward the golden-hour sand tone, lift the shadows so interiors read open and airy, recover highlights so windows keep detail, and push vibrance just enough that the scene looks premium without being over-cooked.
